_comment: D5 fixture for /demos/mcp-apps (LangGraph Python). The MCP-apps demo wires `mcpApps.servers` to https://mcp.excalidraw.com — at request time the runtime's MCP Apps middleware injects the remote MCP server's tools into the agent. When the agent calls one of those tools (here: `create_view`), the middleware fetches the associated UI resource from the MCP server and emits an activity event that the built-in `MCPAppsActivityRenderer` mounts as a sandboxed iframe. The probe (`d5-mcp-apps.ts`) asserts on iframe presence (`[data-testid="mcp-app-iframe"]` or `iframe[sandbox]`). For the test stack to drive that pipeline end-to-end the container must reach mcp.excalidraw.com — the showcase Docker network has outbound internet by default, so this fixture is sufficient. `content` is non-empty (load-bearing) so aimock's Responses-API dispatch routes through `buildContentWithToolCallsResponse` rather than the toolCall-only path that strips reasoning + tool wiring (same constraint as `tool-rendering-reasoning-chain.json`).

match.userMessage: Open Excalidraw and sketch a system diagram

response.reasoning: The user wants a sketch of a client/server/database architecture. I'll call create_view once with three labelled rectangles connected by arrows and a title, framed by a cameraUpdate.
response.content: Sketching a client → server → database diagram in Excalidraw.

match.userMessage: Open Excalidraw and sketch a system diagram

response.content: Sketched a three-box system diagram (Client → Server → Database). Tap the iframe to open it in Excalidraw.

fixtures._comment: Mirror of d5-all.json's mcp-apps suggestion pill #1 ("Draw a flowchart"). Keyed on the distinctive substring "draw a simple flowchart" so a generic feature-parity fixture (e.g. `{userMessage: "steps"}`) doesn't absorb the verbatim pill prompt and break the iframe.

response.reasoning: The user wants a simple three-step flowchart. I'll call create_view once with three labelled rectangles connected by arrows and a title, framed by a cameraUpdate.
response.content: Drawing a simple three-step flowchart in Excalidraw.
Drew a three-step flowchart (Start → Process → End). Tap the iframe to open it in Excalidraw.
